meta.description: V2 LMS causal edge seed — extends the original 38 edges with domains[], source_capability, and target_capability fields per design_doc_v1.2.0. Adds 22 new edges covering funnel_analysis, skill_gap_analysis, behavioral_analysis, training_roi_analysis, and benchmark_analysis intents.
schema_changes_vs_v1: Added: domains (List[str]) — supports multi-domain Qdrant filter
Added: source_capability — abstract capability ID from capabilities/registry.yaml
Added: target_capability — abstract capability ID from capabilities/registry.yaml
Added: version field — '2.0' on all records

edges.mechanism: Higher weekly login frequency leads to higher session-per-user ratios — learners who return regularly drive multi-session engagement patterns
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 1 — Compliance Training Risk

edges.mechanism: The more compliance trainings assigned in a window, the more immediately appear as overdue — assignment load is the primary driver of overdue inventory
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 1 — Compliance Training Risk

edges.mechanism: Larger orgs/cohorts produce higher absolute overdue counts — a confounder that makes large teams look non-compliant when the rate may be equivalent to smaller teams
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 1 — Compliance Training Risk

edges.mechanism: Larger populations produce more absolute missed deadlines — must normalise by cohort size before any cross-org comparison
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 1 — Compliance Training Risk

edges.mechanism: Engaged learners (multiple sessions) complete at higher rates — multi-session patterns indicate content engagement rather than passive checkbox behaviour
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 1 — Compliance Training Risk

edges.mechanism: As overdue accumulates, completion rate rises — but this is deadline pressure completion, not engaged completion; the collider relationship makes this association spurious when conditioning
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 1 — Compliance Training Risk

edges.mechanism: Higher completion rate reduces missed deadlines — the operational translation of completions into deadline management
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 1 — Compliance Training Risk

edges.mechanism: Missed deadlines are the direct accounting input into compliance rate — the strongest edge in the graph and the most proximate lever
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 1 — Compliance Training Risk

edges.mechanism: Missed deadlines accumulate into the annual non-compliance count with a 30-day roll-up lag
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 1 — Compliance Training Risk

edges.mechanism: Intrinsically motivated learners engage more deeply with content before assessment — self-directed learning correlates with higher knowledge retention at time of test
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 2 — Learning Effectiveness Collapse

edges.mechanism: Multiple attempts drive score improvement through learning from failure — the assessment retry cycle is a known mechanism for knowledge consolidation
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 2 — Learning Effectiveness Collapse

edges.mechanism: Structured plan completion ensures prerequisite content is consumed before assessment — controlled sequencing improves average scores by reducing knowledge gaps at test time
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 2 — Learning Effectiveness Collapse

edges.mechanism: Learners in active plans complete evaluations at higher rates — plan structure creates evaluation touchpoints as part of the workflow
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 2 — Learning Effectiveness Collapse

edges.mechanism: Average score is a direct leading indicator of pass rate — when the population mean moves, the pass/fail threshold crossing rate moves proportionally
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 2 — Learning Effectiveness Collapse

edges.mechanism: Distribution shape determines pass rate even when average is stable — a widening distribution (more high scores AND more failures) can mask a growing failure population
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 2 — Learning Effectiveness Collapse

edges.mechanism: Learners who complete evaluations demonstrate post-training engagement — the act of completing an evaluation is itself a retention mechanism (retrieval practice effect)
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 2 — Learning Effectiveness Collapse

edges.mechanism: Mathematical relationship: pass_rate = total_passed / total_assessed; fail_count moves inversely as the complementary metric
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 2 — Learning Effectiveness Collapse

edges.mechanism: Every assigned certification has an expiry timeline — as the certified population grows, the future expiry wave grows proportionally with a 2-3 year lag that manifests as a 60-day expiry cliff
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 3 — Certification Compliance Cliff

edges.mechanism: Fewer instructors → fewer available ILT sessions → waiting list grows as demand exceeds supply; the strongest capacity constraint edge in the graph
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 3 — Certification Compliance Cliff

edges.mechanism: As expiry pressure builds, employees attempt to book ILT renewal sessions — demand surge drives waiting list growth when capacity is fixed
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 3 — Certification Compliance Cliff

edges.mechanism: Structured plan participation creates scheduled ILT touchpoints — plan employees attend ILT sessions at higher rates because attendance is built into their development pathway
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 3 — Certification Compliance Cliff

edges.mechanism: Employees in active plans renew certifications as part of structured milestones — plan participation is a confounder that independently reduces cert overdue counts
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 3 — Certification Compliance Cliff

edges.mechanism: Long waiting lists create registration churn — employees book sessions but cancel when they find earlier alternatives, depressing attendance rate as wait times extend
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 3 — Certification Compliance Cliff

edges.mechanism: ILT attendance is the primary renewal pathway for instructor-led certifications — declining attendance rate directly translates into increasing overdue accumulation
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 3 — Certification Compliance Cliff

edges.mechanism: Platform engagement creates certification awareness — learners who log in regularly respond to expiry notifications and self-schedule; disengaged learners let certs lapse even when seats are available
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 3 — Certification Compliance Cliff

edges.mechanism: Cert overdue count is the direct denominator input into compliance rate — the relationship is near-deterministic with a fortnight reporting lag
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 3 — Certification Compliance Cliff

edges.mechanism: CE credits are the mechanism for many certification renewals — overdue certifications that require CE hours drive down CE compliance rate as the same population fails both metrics
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 3 — Certification Compliance Cliff

edges.mechanism: Vendor cost per hour × total ILT hours = the fixed cost floor; vendor rate increases flow directly into total spend
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 4 — Training ROI Collapse

edges.mechanism: Late cancellations for ILT sessions incur full-cost charges from vendors — each cancellation wastes the committed budget without generating a completion
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 4 — Training ROI Collapse

edges.mechanism: Sessions waiting for approval cannot be offered for registration — pending approval backlog reduces the available seat pool and depresses avg capacity utilisation
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 4 — Training ROI Collapse

edges.mechanism: Learners on waiting lists book backup sessions when seats open — when their primary session arrives, they no-show because they already completed an alternative session
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 4 — Training ROI Collapse

edges.mechanism: Waiting list pressure creates registration churn — learners book seats then cancel, creating gaps that don't fill before session start; this inflates registered-capacity appearance while actual fill rate drops
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 4 — Training ROI Collapse

edges.mechanism: Total committed cost amortised over completions — when cost grows faster than completions, per-learner cost rises
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 4 — Training ROI Collapse

edges.mechanism: Every no-show reduces the completion denominator while leaving costs fixed — the most direct lever on cost-per-completion efficiency
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 4 — Training ROI Collapse

edges.mechanism: No-shows directly reduce attendance rate — one of two independent paths into the collider
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 4 — Training ROI Collapse

edges.mechanism: Waiting list churn (cancel-and-rebook behaviour) independently reduces attendance rate — the second independent path into the collider
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 4 — Training ROI Collapse

edges.mechanism: More completions per fixed cost base directly reduces cost-per-learner — the primary lever for ROI improvement without cutting budget
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 4 — Training ROI Collapse

edges.mechanism: Cost-per-completion drops as completions increase against a near-fixed vendor cost base
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case 4 — Training ROI Collapse

edges.mechanism: Assigned learners convert to started at a rate determined by programme visibility, nudge campaigns, and assignment urgency.
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case — Funnel Analysis

edges.mechanism: Higher started count relative to assigned count reveals the dropout rate at each subsequent stage — low start count inflates the perceived drop rate at completion.
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case — Funnel Analysis

edges.mechanism: High dropout at any funnel stage directly reduces the number of learners reaching certification, compounding across stages.
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case — Funnel Analysis

edges.mechanism: Larger assignment cohorts have higher absolute drop counts — when assignment volume spikes, funnel conversion rates tend to decline due to capacity and attention constraints.
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case — Funnel Analysis

edges.mechanism: Higher total completions feed into the certification pipeline as a prerequisite. Completion is a necessary but not sufficient condition for certification.
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case — Funnel Analysis

edges.mechanism: Increasing the number of required skills for a role reduces the match rate in the short term — the denominator grows while the numerator (completed curricula) remains fixed until training is delivered.
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case — Skill Gap Analysis

edges.mechanism: Each completed curriculum evidences one or more required skills, directly increasing the role-skill match rate numerator.
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case — Skill Gap Analysis

edges.mechanism: Higher overall completion rate feeds the curriculum completion pipeline — learners who complete individual modules also progress through multi-module curricula at higher rates.
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case — Skill Gap Analysis

edges.mechanism: Curricula with embedded assessments are gated — learners who pass assessments advance to the next module and ultimately complete the full curriculum.
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case — Skill Gap Analysis

edges.mechanism: Learners with longer average sessions consume more content per visit, leading to higher completion rates within the same calendar window.
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case — Behavioral Analysis

edges.mechanism: Longer sessions indicate deeper content engagement, which correlates with better knowledge retention and higher assessment scores.
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case — Behavioral Analysis

edges.mechanism: Effective nudge campaigns with high response rates increase weekly login frequency in the 3-7 day window after nudge delivery.
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case — Behavioral Analysis

edges.mechanism: Learners who re-attempt assessments — especially after reviewing content — pass at higher rates on subsequent attempts, improving aggregate pass rate.
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case — Behavioral Analysis

edges.mechanism: All else equal, higher spend reduces the ROI ratio. ROI denominator is total spend — cost efficiency improvement (lower spend for same outcome) directly lifts the ratio.
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case — Training ROI Analysis

edges.mechanism: Higher compliance rates reduce audit-finding risk and associated remediation costs, increasing the measurable value of the training investment and lifting the ROI numerator.
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case — Training ROI Analysis

edges.mechanism: Higher cost per learner reduces ROI ratio, all else equal. Optimising delivery modality or vendor rates improves ROI by compressing the per-learner cost.
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case — Training ROI Analysis

edges.mechanism: Programmes with high pass rates deliver verifiable skill acquisition, which can be translated into reduced error rates or improved productivity — both are measurable ROI numerator inputs.
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case — Training ROI Analysis

edges.mechanism: Current-period compliance_rate directly determines the YoY delta when compared with the same period prior year.
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case — Benchmark Analysis

edges.mechanism: A positive YoY completion delta (improving) correlates with declining annual non-compliance counts as more learners complete before deadlines.
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case — Benchmark Analysis

edges.mechanism: Learners with consistently longer sessions are more likely to complete all required modules within the policy window, directly lifting the compliance rate.
edges.provenance: LMS Use Case — Behavioral Analysis
